message:
fix(transfer): split retrieval excludes from upload excludes to preserve artifact roots

The previous commit (a5119e2) injected get_effective_excludes() into the
retrieval rsync, which fixed the .beads/recovery_* overflow. However,
get_effective_excludes() also includes upload-only hygiene patterns like
"target/", "build/", "dist/", and "node_modules/" — directories that are
the very artifact roots the retrieval command is supposed to download.
This caused silent data loss: rch reported success but the retrieved
directory was missing all compiled outputs.

Split the exclude logic into two methods:

get_effective_excludes() (upload-side, unchanged):
- Full project hygiene: runtime scratch, .rchignore, AND upload-only
  patterns (target/, dist/, node_modules/, coverage caches, etc.)

get_retrieval_excludes() (new, retrieval-side):
- Only runtime scratch guards (REMOTE_RUNTIME_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS) plus
  project-local .rchignore patterns. Intentionally narrower — retrieval
  must still descend into target/ and build/ to collect compiled outputs.

Both build_retrieve_command() and build_download_command() now use
get_retrieval_excludes() instead of get_effective_excludes().

Test updates:
- Rename test to test_build_retrieve_command_applies_rchignore_excludes_
  before_directory_include to reflect the narrower scope.
- Add assertion: retrieval filters must NOT inherit the upload-only
  "target/" exclusion, proving artifact roots remain traversable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
